summary: On Day 3 after wisdom tooth removal, mild soreness exists while lacking other concerning signs such as temperature rise or noticeable puffiness. Initial response involves careful mouth rinses plus soothing medications for symptom management until further evaluation shows improvement or deterioration necessitating additional treatments. Agent2 recommends starting conservatively by addressing potential minor irritation directly via light oral cleansing techniques alongside appropriate pain control strategies. This approach allows time for natural healing processes to take effect under close monitoring. If conditions do not improve within expected timeframe, escalation may become advisable based upon evolving findings during follow-up assessments.
